name: South Texas Duck Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 3 Ducks, mallard-size filleted and cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring water to a boil in a 1-1/2 gallon pot while preparing other ingredients.
2. Heat oil over medium heat in a large skillet. Shake flour and seasonings in a bag; add 10-12 pieces of duck and shake until lightly floured. Brown in the preheated pan for 5 minutes. Do not overcook.
3. Remove duck from the pan, drain on paper towel. Repeat until all meat is browned. Discard remaining flour.
4. Saute vegetables in the meat skillet until onions brown lightly. Add duck and stir-fry for 10 minutes.
5. Dissolve cubes in boiling water. Add chicken rice soup, tomatoes, and seasonings. Add sauted vegetables and duck. Cover and cook for 45 minutes at low to medium heat.
6. To make roux, heat oil to medium hot in a skillet. Sprinkle 1/4 cup flour into oil and stir. Continue adding flour a little at a time until the flour/oil mixture is dry (10-12 minutes) and light brown. Do not burn. Remove from heat. Cool for 5 minutes.
7. Add 2 cups hot duck soup and stir over heat until the mixture thickens. Return roux to soup after the soup has cooked for 45 minutes. Cover and cook for an additional 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat, set aside for 20 minutes.
8. Serve over short-grain rice.
